items.name: Server
items.description: gRPC server for zero-http. Intercepts HTTP/2 streams with `content-type: application/grpc`, routes by `:path` pseudo-header (`/package.Service/Method`), and dispatches to registered service handlers. Handles all four gRPC call types: - Unary (single request → single response) - Server streaming (single request → multiple responses) - Client streaming (multiple requests → single response) - Bidirectional streaming (multiple requests ↔ multiple responses) Supports interceptors (server-side middleware), deadline enforcement, message size limits, and graceful shutdown with call draining.

examples.title: Server streaming
Bidirectional streaming with interceptors

methods.description: Register a service with its handlers.

methodParams.notes: Name of the service as defined in the proto file.
Map of method names to handler functions.

methodOptions.notes: Max incoming message size in bytes.

methods.description: Add a global interceptor that runs before every gRPC call. Interceptors receive `(call, next)` and must call `next()` to continue.
Handle an incoming HTTP/2 stream. Determines if it's a gRPC call, routes to the correct handler, and manages the call lifecycle.

methodParams.notes: HTTP/2 headers from the stream event.

methodReturns.description: `true` if this was handled as a gRPC call.

methods.description: Begin draining — reject new calls and wait for active calls to finish.

methodParams.notes: Maximum time to wait in ms.

methods.description: Get all registered routes for introspection.

methodReturns.type: { method: string, path: string, type: string }[]

examples.code:
registry.routes();
  // [{ method: 'GRPC', path: '/myapp.Greeter/SayHello', type: 'unary' }]

items.name: Client
items.description: Zero-dependency gRPC client using Node.js `http2.connect()`. Supports all four call types (unary, server-streaming, client-streaming, bidirectional), metadata, deadlines, automatic reconnection, and keep-alive.

examples.title: Unary call
Server streaming
Bidirectional streaming
With TLS and metadata

methods.description: Make a unary gRPC call — send one message, receive one response.

methodParams.notes: RPC method name as defined in the proto service.

examples.code: const reply = await client.call('SayHello', { name: 'World' });

methods.description: Make a server-streaming gRPC call — send one request, receive a stream of responses. Returns an async-iterable that yields decoded response messages.
Make a client-streaming gRPC call — send a stream of requests, receive one response. Returns a writable object with `write()`, `end()`, and a `response` Promise.

methodReturns.type: { write: Function, end: Function, response: Promise<object> }

methods.description: Make a bidirectional streaming gRPC call — send and receive streams simultaneously. Returns an object that is both writable (`write`/`end`) and async-iterable.

methodReturns.type: AsyncIterable<object> & { write: Function, end: Function, cancel: Function }

methods.description: Close the client connection.
Check if the client is connected.

items.name: Call Objects
items.description: gRPC call objects for the four RPC patterns. Wraps HTTP/2 streams with protobuf encode/decode, metadata, framing, deadline enforcement, and cancellation support. - `UnaryCall` — single request, single response - `ServerStreamCall` — single request, stream of responses - `ClientStreamCall` — stream of requests, single response - `BidiStreamCall` — bidirectional streaming

examples.title: Unary handler
Server streaming handler
Bidirectional streaming handler

params.notes: The HTTP/2 stream for this call.
Method descriptor from the proto schema.

methods.description: Send initial response metadata (HTTP/2 headers). Must be called before writing any messages. If not called explicitly, headers are sent automatically on the first write.

methodParams.notes: Additional metadata to merge into the response headers.

methods.description: Send a gRPC status and close the call. Trailers carry `grpc-status` and optionally `grpc-message`.
Send an error status and close the call. Convenience wrapper around `sendStatus`.
Write a response message. The object is encoded to protobuf, framed, and sent on the HTTP/2 stream.

methodParams.notes: JavaScript object matching the output message schema.

methodReturns.description: `false` if the stream is not writable.

methods.description: Check if the call has been cancelled.
Cancel the call from the server side.
End the server stream with OK status.
End the bidirectional stream with OK status.

items.name: Proto3 Parser
items.description: Zero-dependency proto3 parser — reads `.proto` file text and produces message descriptors, enum definitions, and service/RPC declarations that the codec and server use at runtime. Supports: - `syntax = "proto3";` - `package`, `option`, `import` (recorded but not resolved) - Scalar types, enums, nested messages, `oneof`, `map`, `repeated` - Services with unary, server-streaming, client-streaming, and bidi RPCs - Comments (// and /* ... *​/) - Reserved fields and field options like `[deprecated = true]`

options.notes: Whether to recursively parse imported files.

returns.description: Parsed schema with messages, enums, and services.

methods.description: Parse a `.proto` file from disk.

examples.code: const schema = parseProtoFile('./protos/chat.proto');

items.name: Protobuf Codec
items.description: Zero-dependency Protocol Buffers wire-format encoder/decoder. Implements the proto3 binary encoding for all scalar types, nested messages, repeated fields, maps, oneofs, and enums. Wire types: - 0: Varint (int32, int64, uint32, uint64, sint32, sint64, bool, enum) - 1: 64-bit fixed (fixed64, sfixed64, double) - 2: Length-delimited (string, bytes, nested message, packed repeated) - 5: 32-bit fixed (fixed32, sfixed32, float)

methods.description: Write raw bytes.
Write a varint (variable-length integer) using LEB128 encoding. Handles values up to 2^53 safely (JavaScript number precision limit).
Write a signed varint using ZigZag encoding (sint32/sint64).
Write a 32-bit fixed integer (little-endian).
Write a 32-bit signed fixed integer (little-endian).
Write a 64-bit fixed integer (little-endian) from a BigInt or number.
Write a 64-bit signed fixed integer (little-endian).
Write a 32-bit IEEE 754 float.
Write a 64-bit IEEE 754 double.
Write a boolean as a single-byte varint.
Write a UTF-8 string (length-prefixed).
Write raw bytes (length-prefixed).
Write a field tag (field number + wire type).
Finalize and return the complete encoded buffer.
Get the total byte size of all written data.
Read a varint (LEB128-encoded variable-length integer). Returns a JavaScript number (safe for values up to 2^53).
Read a signed varint using ZigZag decoding (sint32/sint64).
Read a 32-bit fixed unsigned integer (little-endian).
Read a 32-bit fixed signed integer (little-endian).
Read a 64-bit fixed unsigned integer (little-endian). Returns a number (precision loss above 2^53).
Read a 64-bit fixed signed integer (little-endian).
Read a 32-bit IEEE 754 float.
Read a 64-bit IEEE 754 double.
Read a boolean varint.
Read a length-prefixed UTF-8 string.
Read length-prefixed raw bytes.
Read a field tag and decode field number + wire type.
Skip a field value based on its wire type (for unknown fields).
Create a sub-reader for a length-delimited embedded message.

methodReturns.description: A new Reader limited to the embedded message bytes.

methods.description: Write a string field (tag + length-delimited string).
Write a bytes/embedded-message field (tag + length-delimited bytes).
Write an int32/enum field (tag + varint).
Write a bool field (tag + varint 0/1).
Number of bytes remaining to be read.
Whether all bytes have been consumed.
Current read position (byte offset).
Encode a JavaScript object to protobuf binary using a message descriptor.

methodParams.notes: Message descriptor from the proto parser.
Map of all message descriptors (for nested types).

methods.example: const buf = encode({ name: 'Alice', age: 30 }, personDesc, allMessages);

examples.code: const buf = encode({ name: 'Alice', age: 30 }, personDesc, allMessages);

methods.description: Decode protobuf binary into a JavaScript object using a message descriptor.

methodParams.notes: Message descriptor from the proto parser.

examples.code: const person = decode(buffer, personDesc, allMessages);

methods.description: Check if a protobuf type can be packed (numeric/bool scalars only).

items.name: Status Codes
items.description: Standard gRPC status codes (as defined by the gRPC specification). Each code has a numeric value, a name, and a human-readable description. Used by both server and client to communicate call outcomes via trailers.

methods.description: Get the human-readable name for a gRPC status code.

items.name: Metadata
items.description: gRPC metadata container — typed key-value pairs transmitted as HTTP/2 headers (initial metadata) and trailers (trailing metadata). Keys ending in `-bin` carry binary values (base64-encoded on the wire). All other keys carry ASCII string values.

examples.code:
md.set('icon-bin', Buffer.from([0x89, 0x50, 0x4e, 0x47]));
  md.get('icon-bin');  // <Buffer 89 50 4e 47>
examples.title: Binary metadata

methods.description: Set a metadata key to a single value, replacing any existing values. Binary keys (ending in `-bin`) accept Buffer values; all others accept strings.

examples.code: md.set('x-request-id', 'abc-123');

methods.description: Add a value to a metadata key without replacing existing values. Allows multi-valued metadata (e.g. multiple tags or roles).
Get the first value for a metadata key.

methodReturns.description: First value, or undefined if not set.

examples.code: md.get('x-request-id');  // 'abc-123'

methods.description: Get all values for a metadata key.

methodReturns.description: Array of values (empty if key not set).

examples.code: md.getAll('x-roles');  // ['admin', 'editor']

methods.description: Check whether a metadata key has been set.
Remove a metadata key and all its values.

methodReturns.description: `true` if the key existed and was removed.

methods.description: Remove all metadata entries.
Return the number of distinct metadata keys.
Convert metadata to a plain object suitable for HTTP/2 headers/trailers. Multi-valued keys are joined with `, `. Binary values are base64-encoded.

examples.code:
md.toHeaders();
  // { 'x-request-id': 'abc-123', 'x-roles': 'admin, editor' }

methods.description: Merge entries from another Metadata instance or plain object.
Create a shallow clone of this Metadata.

methodReturns.description: New Metadata instance with the same entries.

methods.description: Return all entries as an array of `[key, value]` pairs.
Return all distinct keys.
Create a Metadata instance from HTTP/2 headers, extracting only user metadata (skipping pseudo-headers, gRPC internal headers, and standard HTTP headers).

examples.code: const md = Metadata.fromHeaders(stream.headers);

methods.description: Check if a metadata key is a binary key (ends with `-bin`).
Normalize a metadata key to lowercase.

items.name: Framing
items.description: gRPC length-prefixed message framing over HTTP/2. Each gRPC message on the wire has a 5-byte header: - Byte 0:   Compression flag (0 = uncompressed, 1 = compressed) - Bytes 1-4: Message length as 32-bit big-endian unsigned integer Followed by the protobuf-encoded message body. Also handles optional gzip compression/decompression.

methods.description: Encode a protobuf message buffer into a gRPC framed message.

examples.code:
const frame = frameEncode(protobufBytes);
  stream.write(frame);
const frame = await frameEncode(protobufBytes, { compress: true });
examples.title: With compression

methods.description: Feed a data chunk to the parser. May trigger zero or more `onMessage` callbacks.

methodParams.notes: Raw bytes from the HTTP/2 stream.

methods.description: Reset the parser state, discarding any buffered data.
Destroy the parser, preventing further processing.

items.name: Health Service
items.description: gRPC Health Checking Protocol implementation (grpc.health.v1.Health). Supports `Check` (unary) and `Watch` (server-stream) RPCs. Required for production deployments behind Kubernetes, Envoy, AWS ALB, and any load balancer that uses standard gRPC health probes.

examples.title: Per-service health status

methods.description: Set the health status for a service.
Get the current status for a service.

methodReturns.description: Status code, or SERVICE_UNKNOWN if not registered.

methods.description: Set all registered services to NOT_SERVING for graceful shutdown.
Handle a Check RPC — unary request for current health of a service.
Handle a Watch RPC — server-stream that pushes status changes. Sends the current status immediately, then pushes on every change.
Get the schema object needed for server registration. Avoids requiring proto parsing — returns descriptors directly.
Get the handler map for server registration. Binds methods to this instance.

items.name: Reflection
items.description: gRPC Server Reflection Protocol implementation (grpc.reflection.v1). Enables `grpcurl`, `grpcui`, Postman, and other debugging tools to introspect registered services without supplying `.proto` files.

methods.description: Register a schema for reflection. Builds and caches the FileDescriptorProto at registration time.

methodParams.notes: Filename to use. Defaults to schema package or '<inline>'.

methods.description: Get the schema for server registration.
Get the handler map.

items.name: Load Balancing
items.description: Client-side load balancing for gRPC. Distributes requests across multiple backend addresses using pick-first (with failover) or round-robin policies. Manages subchannels (HTTP/2 connections) per backend with automatic reconnection, exponential backoff, and health awareness.

methods.description: Connect to the backend.
Get the session, connecting if needed.
Whether this subchannel is ready to serve requests.
Whether this subchannel is considered healthy.
Shut down this subchannel.
Pick the next subchannel.
Pick a subchannel for the next request.
Get an HTTP/2 session from the selected subchannel.
Shut down all subchannels.

items.name: Credentials
items.description: Channel credentials for gRPC connections. Provides factory functions for creating insecure, SSL/TLS, and metadata-based credentials. Supports certificate rotation and credential composition. Uses only Node.js built-in `tls` and `fs` — no external packages.

examples.title: Insecure (plaintext)

examples.code: const creds = ChannelCredentials.createSsl(fs.readFileSync('ca.pem'));
examples.title: Server-only TLS
Mutual TLS (mTLS)
Metadata credentials (e.g. Bearer token)
Composed credentials (TLS + per-call metadata)

methods.description: Create insecure (plaintext) credentials. No TLS — suitable for development or service-mesh environments where transport security is handled by the infrastructure.
Create SSL/TLS credentials.

methodParams.notes:
Root CA certificate(s) in PEM format.
If null, uses the system default trust store.
Client private key in PEM format (for mTLS).
Client certificate in PEM format (for mTLS).

examples.code: const creds = ChannelCredentials.createSsl(fs.readFileSync('ca.pem'));
examples.title: Server-only TLS

examples.code: const creds = ChannelCredentials.createSsl(caPem, keyPem, certPem);
examples.title: Mutual TLS

methods.description: Create SSL credentials from PEM file paths. Files are read once at creation time.

examples.code: const creds = ChannelCredentials.createSslFromFiles('./certs/ca.pem', './certs/client.key', './certs/client.pem');

methods.description: Create per-call metadata credentials. The generator function is called before each RPC to produce metadata headers (e.g. authorization tokens).

methodParams.notes:
`(params?) => object|Promise<object>`.
Returns key-value pairs to merge into call metadata.
`params` includes `{ serviceUrl, methodName }`.

methods.description: Combine multiple credentials into one. At most one channel credential (insecure/SSL) and any number of call credentials (metadata) can be combined.
Whether this credential uses TLS.
Get the TLS connection options for `http2.connect()`. Returns `null` for insecure/metadata-only credentials.

methodReturns.description: TLS options `{ ca, key, cert, rejectUnauthorized }`.

methods.description: Generate per-call metadata by running all metadata generators.

Create SSL credentials with automatic certificate rotation. Watches certificate files for changes and reloads them. Returns a credentials-like object with `getCurrent()` to get the latest credentials and `stop()` to cease watching.

methodOptions.notes: Path to client key file (for mTLS).
Path to client certificate file (for mTLS).

items.name: Proto Watcher
items.description: Proto file hot-reload for development. Watches `.proto` files for changes using `fs.watch()` and re-parses/re-registers gRPC services automatically. **Dev-only** — disabled by default when `NODE_ENV=production`.
